briefdescription.para: The following constants map to the XPAR parameters created in the xparameters.h file.

detaileddescription.para: They are defined here such that a user can easily change all the needed parameters in one place.

briefdescription.para: The ZCU102 and ZCU106 boards use a PMBus MUX address of 0x75 with channel address 0x05 and PMBus slave address 0x13.

detaileddescription.para: The ZC702 and ZC706 boards use a PMBus MUX address of 0x74 with channel address 0x80 and PMBus slave address 0x52.

The KCU105 board uses a PMBus MUX address of 0x74 or 0x75 with channel address 0x04 and PMBus slave address 0x0A. Please refer to the User Guide of the respective board and the PMBus device datasheet for further information about the correct addresses and channel to use for PMBus communication. The defines below must be updated to match the board in use.

briefdescription.para: The following constant defines the address of the PMBus device on the IIC bus.

detaileddescription.para: Note that this is a 7-bit address. The PMBus slave address may vary depending on the board and the PMBus device used. For example, the KCU105 board uses 0x0A as the PMBus slave address for its power controller. Please refer to the User Guide of the respective board and the PMBus device datasheet for further information about the correct slave address to use.

briefdescription.para: This define should be uncommented if there is an IIC MUX on the board to which the PMBus device is connected.

detaileddescription.para: For example, the KCU105 board uses an IIC MUX to access PMBus devices.

briefdescription.para: PMBus READ_VOUT uses LINEAR16 format.

detaileddescription.para: As per PMBus specification, the exponent is defined by VOUT_MODE. For the PMBus device used in this example, VOUT_MODE exponent is fixed at -12 (device/board specific).

briefdescription.para: Maximum delay count used for timeout handling.
Internal IIC event mask.
Internal event value used for IIC event checking.
Shift to get exponent bits.
Mask for 5-bit exponent.
Mask for 11-bit mantissa.
Exponent sign bit.
Mantissa sign bit.
The instance of the IIC device.
Flag to check completion of Transmission.
Flag to check completion of Reception.
Error event flag set by the IIC interrupt handler.
This function initializes the IIC MUX to select the PMBus channel.

inbodydescription.para: Wait for transmit completion.

The TransmitComplete flag is set to 1 by the transmit interrupt handler once all requested bytes are successfully transmitted. Xil_WaitForEvent() polls this software flag until it matches the expected completion value or the timeout expires, preventing an infinite wait in case the interrupt is not serviced.

briefdescription.para: This Send handler is called asynchronously from an interrupt context and indicates that data in the specified buffer has been sent.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the IIC device instance.
is the number of bytes sent.

briefdescription.para: This Receive handler is called asynchronously from an interrupt context and indicates that data in the specified buffer has been received.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the IIC device instance.
is the number of bytes received.

briefdescription.para: This status handler is invoked from the interrupt context to report IIC status events.

detaileddescription.para: It checks for error conditions such as arbitration loss or missing slave acknowledgment and sets an error flag.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the IIC driver instance for which the handler is being called for.
indicates the condition that has occurred.

simplesect.para: The errorflag is used by the application to detect PMBus transaction failures.

briefdescription.para: This function demonstrates PMBus interrupt access: initializes IIC, sets up interrupts, selects MUX (if enabled), and read voltage and temperature from the PMBus device, printing results.

inbodydescription.para: Initialize the IIC driver so that it is ready to use.
Setup the Interrupt System.
Set the Handlers for transmit and reception.
Start the IIC device.
Set the Global Interrupt Enable.
Initialize the IIC MUX on boards where the PMBus devices are connected through the MUX.
Set the Slave address.
Read the output voltage (VOUT) from the PMBus device
Combine the two bytes into a 16-bit raw value, convert it to voltage using Linear16 format, scale it to centivolts, and print the result.
Read the output temperature from the PMBus device
Convert raw PMBus temperature data (Linear11) to centi-degrees Celsius and print

briefdescription.para: This function sends a single command byte to the PMBus slave device using interrupt-driven IIC communication.

parameterdescription.para: contains the command byte to be sent to the slave.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the command was sent successfully.
XST_FAILURE if an error occurred during transmission.

simplesect.para: This function waits for the IIC bus to become free before sending the command and waits for the transmission to complete.

inbodydescription.para: Ensure bus is free before starting
Send the command byte to the slave device
Wait for transmit completion.

The TransmitComplete flag is set to 1 by the transmit interrupt handler once all requested bytes are successfully transmitted. Xil_WaitForEvent() polls this software flag until it matches the expected completion value or the timeout expires, preventing an infinite wait in case the interrupt is not serviced.

briefdescription.para: This function reads a 2-byte word from the PMBus slave device using interrupt-driven IIC communication.

detaileddescription.para: It first sends the command byte, then receives the data.

parameterdescription.para: contains the command byte to be sent to the slave.
is a pointer to the buffer to store the received data.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the word was read successfully.
XST_FAILURE if an error occurred during the operation.

simplesect.para: This function uses a repeated start condition between the write (command) and read (data) phases. It waits for the receive operation to complete or timeout.

inbodydescription.para: Ensure bus is free before starting
PMBus requires a repeated start between command write and data read. Enable repeated start for the command WRITE phase.
Send command byte (write)
Restore options on failure
Disable repeated start BEFORE the READ phase so STOP is generated
Receive 2 bytes (read)
Wait for receive completion.

The ReceiveComplete flag is set to 1 by the receive interrupt handler once all requested bytes are successfully received. Xil_WaitForEvent() polls this software flag until it matches the expected completion value or the timeout expires, preventing an infinite wait in case the interrupt is not serviced.
Make sure bus is free for the next command

briefdescription.para: This function converts a PMBus Linear11 formatted value to float.

parameterdescription.para: is the 16-bit raw value from the PMBus device.

inbodydescription.para: Step 1: Extract the exponent and mantissa fields.
LINEAR11 format: Bits[15:11] : 5-bit signed exponent Bits[10:0] : 11-bit signed mantissa
Step 2: Sign-extend the 5-bit exponent. Bit[4] (0x10) is the sign bit of the exponent(2's complement).
Step 3: Sign-extend the 11-bit mantissa. Bit[10] (0x400) is the sign bit of the mantissa(2's complement).
Step 4: Apply the Linear11 conversion formula. Value = Mantissa × 2^Exponent

briefdescription.para: This function converts a PMBus Linear16 formatted value to float.

parameterdescription.para: is the 16-bit raw value from the PMBus device.

inbodydescription.para: Step 1: Convert the 5‑bit exponent to a signed integer. Bit[4] (0x10) is the sign bit in a 5‑bit two's‑complement exponent.
Step 2: Apply the Linear16 conversion formula. Value = Raw × 2^Exponent

briefdescription.para: Write buffer used for IIC data transmission.

detaileddescription.para: Main function to call the PMBus interrupt example.

inbodydescription.para: Run the PMBus example.

briefdescription.para: This file consists of an interrupt mode design example which uses the Xilinx IIC device and low-level driver to communicate with a PMBus device.

detaileddescription.para: The example demonstrates how to read voltage and temperature from a PMBus-compliant device using the IIC interface. It includes conversion routines for PMBus Linear11 and Linear16 formats.
The XIic_MasterSend() API is used to transmit the data and XIic_MasterRecv() API is used to receive the data. Interrupt handlers are used for send, receive, and status events.
The define PMBUS_SLAVE_ADDR in this file needs to be changed depending on the PMBus device and board configuration on which this example is to be run.
This code assumes that no Operating System is being used.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
3.15 vlt 02/18/26 Initial version
